mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market and strong economic activity, which typically translates into healthy corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE is below 5.0% over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a critical threshold, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, which increases borrowing costs and dampens economic growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: This hypothesis fails if the S&P 500 shows positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of periods where FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong growth in retail sales and non-farm payrolls indicates robust consumer demand and employment, signaling a healthy economic expansion that drives corporate revenue and profits, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and PAYEMS show recent positive trends
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the Rank IC for this combined signal falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during periods of economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts signals a weakening housing market, which often precedes or accompanies broader economic slowdowns, negatively impacting consumer confidence and investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average declines by more than 10% from its 12-month peak
failure_condition: This mechanism fails if S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a confirmed HOUST decline trigger during identified housing downturns.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth turns negative on a year-over-year basis, it indicates a contraction in the manufacturing and mining sectors, signaling weakening supply-side activity and overall economic health, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and consumer purchasing power, while also prompting central bank tightening, which collectively pressures equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower during periods of CPIAUCSL above 3% YoY compared to periods below 3% YoY (t-test p > 0.1), this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment and a high federal funds rate signifies a late-cycle economy where the central bank is actively tightening monetary policy to cool an overheating labor market and inflation, which typically precedes an economic slowdown and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of t-1mo
failure_condition: This hypothesis fails if the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances when both UNRATE is below 5.0% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below a minimal threshold, it indicates a significant deceleration in overall economic activity, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and a subsequent decline in S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are not statistically significantly negative (p > 0.05) following periods where GDPC1 growth is below 1.0%.
